questions.question: Explain the working principle of a barcode reader.
keyPoints: Barcode reader uses light source (LED or laser)
Light is reflected differently by bars and spaces
Dark bars absorb light, light spaces reflect more
Photodiode converts reflected light into electrical signals
Decoder interprets signals into readable data

questions.question: Differentiate between OCR, OMR, and MICR technologies.
keyPoints: OCR: Converts printed text images to editable text using light scanning
OMR: Detects marks/bubbles on paper for multiple-choice tests and surveys
MICR: Uses magnetically charged ink for character recognition, mainly in banking
OCR reads characters; OMR reads marks; MICR reads magnetic ink

questions.question: List four pointing devices and state one application of each.
keyPoints: Mouse: General computer operation and navigation
Trackball: CAD applications and limited workspace environments
Joystick: Gaming and flight simulators
Light Pen: Direct screen selection in CAD and interactive displays
Touch Screen: Smartphones, kiosks, and tablets
Graphics Tablet: Digital art and design work

questions.question: What are biometric input devices? Mention two examples and their applications.
keyPoints: Biometric devices verify identity using physical characteristics
Fingerprint scanner: Used in smartphones, attendance systems, and security
Iris scanner: Used in high-security areas like airports and banks
Facial recognition: Used in smartphones and surveillance
Voice recognition: Used for authentication and voice assistants

questions.question: Describe the functioning of a keyboard as an input device.
keyPoints: Keyboard contains keys representing characters and commands
Each key has a switch mechanism
Pressing a key completes a circuit
Controller detects the key position and generates scan code
Scan code is converted to ASCII or Unicode for the computer
Keyboard sends data serially to the computer via USB or PS/2

questions.question: Explain the working of a scanner with special reference to flatbed scanner.
keyPoints: Scanner uses light-sensitive sensors (CCD or CIS)
Light source illuminates the document
Reflection is captured by sensors
Document is placed on glass bed for flatbed scanner
Stepper motor moves the scanning head across the document
Analog signals are converted to digital data
Image processing software creates digital image

questions.question: Explain different types of input devices used in computer systems. Give examples and applications of each category.
keyPoints: Pointing devices: Mouse, Trackball, Joystick, Light Pen - for navigation and selection
Text input devices: Keyboard - for entering text and commands
Scanning devices: Scanner, Barcode reader, OCR - for document and data entry
Audio input devices: Microphone - for voice and sound input
Biometric devices: Fingerprint, Iris scanner - for authentication
Specialized devices: Graphics tablet, Digital camera - for specific tasks

questions.question: Describe the classification of input devices in detail with suitable examples.
keyPoints: Manual input devices: Keyboard, Mouse, Joystick - require human intervention
Scanning devices: Scanner, OMR, OCR, Barcode reader - convert pre-recorded data
Audio input devices: Microphone, MIDI keyboard - sound-based input
Biometric devices: Fingerprint scanner, Face recognition - identity verification
Pointing devices: Mouse, Trackball, Touch screen - cursor control
Digital input devices: Digital camera, Video capture - direct digital input

questions.question: What is the difference between mechanical mouse and optical mouse? Explain the working of an optical mouse.
keyPoints: Mechanical mouse uses a rolling ball; optical mouse uses light sensor
Mechanical mouse requires cleaning; optical mouse has no moving parts
Optical mouse is more accurate and responsive
Optical mouse working: LED or laser illuminates surface
Sensor captures images of surface at high frequency
DSP compares successive images to calculate movement
Movement data is sent to computer via USB

questions.question: Discuss the importance of input devices in computer systems. Explain any five input devices with their applications.
keyPoints: Input devices bridge human understanding and machine processing
Without input devices, computers cannot receive user instructions
They enable data entry, commands, and interaction
Five devices: Keyboard (text entry), Mouse (pointing), Scanner (digitizing), Microphone (audio), Touch screen (direct interaction)
Each device serves specific purposes and user needs
